summary: Captured New Republic agent Shael Edur, designated Prisoner C-1125, is subjected to psychological interrogation aboard the Imperial vessel Inquisitor. An ISB agent uses sensory deprivation, isolation, and the claim that the New Republic has abandoned her to break her resistance, then coerces her into naming her co-conspirators by threatening to harm others. After she gives up the names of four Marines and six Corellians, she is rewarded with officer-grade food, which she eats with revulsion before collapsing into sobbing despair.

title: RPlog:Reckoning
summary: In their Imperial cell at night, the recovering Tokoga and Myiari reflect on Brek's stinging insults and the morality of the war. Myiari shares her painful backstory as a former Imperial citizen on Commenor whose eldest brother was killed by Imperial crossfire, and her guilt over the stormtroopers she has killed. The two grow closer and begin plotting an escape, planning for Myiari to disarm and overpower the next stormtrooper who enters so they can break out.

title: RPlog:Reclaiming Coruscant
summary: The climactic Imperial fleet assault to retake Coruscant from the New Republic, while the NR conducts a staged evacuation under Operation Anti-Twilight. Massive capital-ship and starfighter combat ensues; the Empire breaks the NR picket line with bomber wings while NR pilots cover fleeing refugee freighters. A New Republic Marine (Vengan Draelis) infiltrates a Victory-class Star Destroyer via a torpedo pod and rigs it to blow, while the Imperials destroy the NRSD Monarch. The Empire wins the orbital space, retaking Coruscant, but the New Republic succeeds in evacuating most refugees, retreating at a cost of roughly 40-50% of its force versus the Empire's ~30-35%.

title: RPlog:Recon into Dac
summary: An Imperial recon team aboard the light cruiser Minnow conducts a covert sensor sweep of the Mon Calamari (Dac) system to map New Republic naval assets and shipyard defenses. Lt. Vextin Mandor and pilot Liza Molokai chat at length about her Sarian heritage before being detected by New Republic commander Rasi Praddhan Cen aboard the Audacity, who deploys Ghost Squadron fighters (Kesander Beysarus, Raxis L'ygr) to intercept. Mandor stalls with political bluster about civilian rights while Molokai completes the scans and escapes to Caspar despite heavy damage to the ship.

title: RPlog:Recovery
summary: In the New Alderaan base med bay, Ambassador Ambrosia Delgard, her daughter Gabi, and the defecting Imperial officer Lt Liora Kovani recover from their ordeal aboard the ISD Nemesis. Gabi fusses over bacta treatment while Kovani gently befriends her, and the adults work through the tension of Kovani's uncertain status, with Ambrosia insisting she will not be jailed but will stay with them. The scene reveals Kovani is Ambrosia's estranged daughter and ends with the three heading off together to find a shower, ice cream in hand.

title: RPlog:Reenlistment
summary: At Zubindi Hakoon's Silverwood House of Fashion on Caspar, Presav Crestian Tarasar and Captain Sadim Gnik (Lynae's half-brother) meet Doctor Lynae Cassius to recruit her into the Caspian Navy ahead of wargames at Charros IV. They present a hand-carved box containing a tailored admiral's uniform. Over Ithorian brandy, Crestian explains the politics of the Caspian Union, its mutual-defense pact with the Imperium, and the roles of Axel and Darth Malign, ultimately convincing Lynae to accept the commission and the rank of Admiral.

title: RPlog:Reflections and Revelations
summary: On a New Republic base on Mon Calamari, Paul Nighman plays a sad song on Shenner's guitar at dawn, accidentally waking Jessalyn Valios. The two reconcile after their recent argument, each apologizing, and acknowledge how much they have in common. The conversation grows tender and charged with new mutual attraction before Paul departs for a walk, leaving Jessalyn blushing at his parting compliment.

title: RPlog:Regroup and Reconvene
summary: Aboard the casino ship Empress of the Stars, Lynae Cassius hosts a farewell dinner for Darth Malus (Lorn Rhys) to compare their separate, failed meetings with the Force-user Johanna. Malus reports Johanna grew hostile and physically attacked him, telekinetically pinning him, and concludes she is near falling to the dark side and could be goaded but not controlled. He critiques Lynae for using his bounty-marked name in front of Malif Tal-Tahn and a holo-influencer; she apologizes. Malus then redirects Lynae's anti-Force research toward understanding and amplifying the Force rather than eradicating it, and shows her the fully outfitted medbay, lab, and quarters he built for her aboard the Revenant. They agree to rendezvous on Tatooine to meet Johanna again, with Malus planning secret contingencies.

title: RPlog:Rehab Discovery
summary: Luke Skywalker visits singer Serenella Jordain, who is in rehab at Remembrance Hospital on New Alderaan after a spice addiction and a spaceport fight; her injured pilot friend Raxis L'ygr is present. Serenella reveals she was married to Antoine Dareus, whom she believes died after being poisoned in CSA custody, as Axel Vichten told her. Luke is shocked because he saw Dareus alive aboard the Malevolence less than a month earlier, and realizes Dareus has been turned/brainwashed by the Sith. He promises to investigate whether Dareus was truly poisoned and offers Serenella hope while encouraging her recovery.

title: RPlog:Relocation, relocation, relocation
summary: Imperial Security Bureau agent Alysha Mara, alongside Major Calyx Drogyn and Captain Sanoire Cror, oversees the forced relocation of an entire Bothan town near Holm'starn on Bothawui under the pretext of a smuggling crackdown. When Bothan resistance fighters detonate hidden mines against the marching column, Drogyn issues a weapons-free order and the operation turns into a massacre, with stormtroopers gunning down civilians, gassing buildings, and TIE bombers leveling the town. The town is utterly destroyed, its inhabitants killed or deported, with only a quarter making it onto the prisoner transports.

title: RPlog:Renaissance of Innovation
summary: In the renovated Union Council Chambers on Caspar, Presav Crestian Tarasar and Vice Presav Cas Mos host a landmark address proclaiming a Second Renaissance of Innovation, anchored by new ship designs from the Xi Char and Haor Chall Engineering. Councillor ak unveils four new starfighter classes (Vulture Mk IV, Guardian, Vector, Apex), plus Baron droids and Hailfire droid tanks, with a flyby over Plaxton. The event gathers Caspian and Imperium dignitaries including Axel Vichten, Grand Moff Elana Tracer, and the socialite Nara, and announces upcoming wargames at Charros IV.

title: RPlog:Rendezvous with Karrde
summary: Aboard Orson Tighe's smuggler ship at an asteroid, Simon Sezirok seeks Drew Carvallo's advice on winning Jessalyn Valios over before Orson brings in Talon Karrde to discuss destroying the Death Star. Simon and Jessalyn argue that the New Republic cannot succeed without the Jedi to fight the Sith aboard the station, but Karrde rebukes their overconfidence, noting they bring no plan and cannot even locate the Death Star. The meeting collapses without an alliance; Karrde walks away, leaving the two Jedi to seek out Luke Skywalker instead.

title: RPlog:Reporting Lance
summary: After limping home to Ord Mantell in a battle-scarred X-wing, Raxis L'ygr reports to Lt. Colonel Ridge Archilles that his wingman Lance Corbet has been captured by the Empire during a patrol near quadrant 33. As Raxis fills out the official report, Ridge questions him about the lone TIE that ambushed them and listens to Raxis's flight recording. Ridge recognizes the pilot's voice as 'Wolf,' one of Krieg's pilots, giving them a lead on where Lance is held. Raxis asks to join the rescue but declines Ridge's offer to sneak him aboard, insisting on going only with his CO Major Sh'vani's approval.

title: RPlog:Republic Senate Session
summary: The New Republic Senate convenes in the Senate Chambers on Coruscant, chaired by Leia Organa Solo. Senator Lemos Ackinbrac of Dac moves a resolution condemning Imperial occupation and committing the military to liberate occupied worlds, with General Ikihsa Enb'Zik testifying; the resolution passes. Enb'Zik then delivers an impassioned oration in favor of a bill restoring voting rights to delegates from Imperial-occupied planets, invoking the founding Declaration of the New Republic. Despite a security objection, the bill also passes with a strong majority before Leia calls a recess.

title: RPlog:Rescue of Danik Kreldin
summary: Imperial Intelligence briefs a task force that Advisor Danik Kreldin was abducted on Corellia by the Dark Jedi Johanna Siri te Danaan and is being held on Tatooine, prompting a rescue led by the 501st Legion and supported by Navy pilots. Imperial ground forces lock down Mos Eisley spaceport and confront the smuggler Kyokusha Gackt, who tries to bargain Kreldin's location for 50,000 credits, while Johanna uses the Force to mind-control Sergeant Tanis Cadell into attacking pilot Krieg Inrokana, who is gravely wounded. Johanna cuts her way through troopers, hijacks Kyokusha's freighter Talisman (unknowingly with the captured Danik aboard), and flees as TIE squadrons cripple the ship. The damaged freighter crashes in the dunes while medic Lynae Cassius-Caiton stabilizes the mortally wounded Krieg with biofoam.

title: RPlog:Rescued In Transit
summary: While being transported sedated and shackled aboard the NR prison shuttle Justice toward a penal world, Seifer Wolf is unexpectedly freed when Tritus Vaticus, Lierra Carlon, and the Twi'lek Chez'na Navul'nu ambush the ship with a rocket launcher near Caluula, crashing it into the jungle. The black-clad mercenaries board and execute the surviving marines and guards, including the sadistic Sgt Tycho. Tritus and Lierra dispute over who claims Wolf, briefly coming to blows, before Tritus frees Wolf from his restraints; Wolf agrees to submit to Lord Draga's authority and the group prepares to fly him to Tatooine.

title: RPlog:Resign or Retire
summary: Admiral Lynae Cassius visits Presav Crestian Tarasar's office in Plaxton to formally leave the Caspian Navy, because her close association with the renegade Darth Malus has made her a security risk to the Union. After philosophical conversation about history, their children, and Sith ideals of balance, Crestian confirms her resignation is necessary but not vindictive, reassuring her that her son Andrev may keep his place at the academy. Lynae chooses to retire cleanly, surrenders her service gear, and the two part on respectful terms.

title: RPlog:Retribution - A Risky Plan for the Orphans
summary: Receiving a garbled message from pilot Aramis Tyrese about orphaned children stranded in a Coruscant government building during the Imperial blockade, Luke Skywalker flies Jedi One on a dangerous run from blockaded Coruscant to the NRSD Reprisal. Captain Lynae Caiton of the ISC Broadsword taunts and pursues him, but the Force-guided X-Wing evades her firing solutions and lands safely. Luke and Aramis then devise a risky plan to evacuate the Wing C orphans using Brandis's and other Jedi ships (carrying artifacts and the comatose Jedi Sabrina) as cover, with starfighter escorts drawing Imperial attention. Aramis is tasked with prepping the operation.

title: RPlog:Retribution - Defending the Malevolence
summary: As part of the Retribution campaign over Coruscant, the New Republic launches a bold probing strike against the Imperial flagship Super Star Destroyer Malevolence to test its defenses and gather intelligence. NR carracks and corvettes (Red Star, Jack Cade, and escorts) push the Fusillade toward the Malevolence, screened by X-wing and A-wing fighters, while Imperial fighters under Jal'Dana Rall and the Broadsword/Prowler defend. In a personal grudge-duel, Imperial pilot Krieg Inrokana mortally wounds and kills NR A-wing pilot Seth Skyler. The Imperials concentrate fire on the Carrack Red Star and tear it apart; the heavily-outmatched NR strike force withdraws after damaging the Malevolence, having achieved its intelligence-gathering goal.

title: RPlog:Retribution - Evacuation of the Orphans
summary: During the Imperial invasion of Coruscant, New Republic personnel including Luke Skywalker, Marines Vengan Draelis and Aramis Tyrese, and the Chyleni pilot Kyrin Sh'vani evacuate a group of orphaned children from a government complex near the bombed Senate. The operation is briefly disrupted by an Ewok named Munch who, misreading the situation, believes the children are captives and attempts to steal a speeder to rescue them. The children, including Arise Omondra, are loaded aboard armored speeders and successfully flown out as Luke reflects on the darkness behind the evacuation.

title: RPlog:Retribution - Jedi and Sith Duel
summary: Following the Force's promptings, Jedi Master Luke Skywalker waits in the ruins of the Senate Building on Coruscant for Sith Warlord Vassily Korolov, who arrives with a stormtrooper escort seeking vengeance for his slain master. The two engage in an epic lightsaber duel that ranges across multiple Force-combat techniques, during which Korolov fires concealed energy beams from his bone mask, searing Luke's eyes. Temporarily blinded, Luke escapes by Force-leaping into a nearby ruined building while Korolov, believing the Jedi dealt with, orders the 501st to full offense. Several stormtroopers are killed during the clash.

title: RPlog:Retribution - Protecting Jedi Artifacts
summary: The morning after the Senate Building's destruction, an exhausted Luke Skywalker sets a plan in motion to evacuate Jedi artifacts (journals, data-disks and a holocron) from the Jedi apartment on Coruscant before the Empire can seize them. He rendezvouses with A-wing pilot Aramis Tyrese and Marine Vengan Draelis, while Kyrin Sh'vani and apprentice Brandis Finian transport the shielded artifact case by speeder. When CorGuard agents attack the nearby Blockworth Garrison and intercept the group, a running speeder chase erupts; Luke boards an enemy CorGuard speeder and cuts down its stormtroopers. The scene also reveals Luke's realization that Senator Faust was a hidden Sith Lord cloaking Coruscant in the dark side.

title: RPlog:Retribution - The Empire Wedges In
summary: As Grand Admiral Danik Kreldin culminates the years-long Imperial Blitzkrieg with an assault on Coruscant, Imperial Task Force Hammer attempts to breach the New Republic 2nd Fleet's battle line in the Coruscant sector to deploy ground support. The ISC Broadsword under Captain Lynae Caiton uses a micro-jump and below-plane maneuver to engage the NRSD Reprisal under Captain David Stone, while massed turbolaser fire and fighter dogfights rage between Imperial squadrons and New Republic Falcon/Ghost/Dagger squadrons. Kreldin personally flies his crimson TIE Interceptor to duel Ghost One (Johmac Mabru). The Broadsword succeeds in preventing the Reprisal from reaching Engagement Area Phantom, and Task Force Hammer drives a wedge into the 2nd Fleet line and begins deploying troops to Coruscant's surface.

title: RPlog:Retribution - The Fall of Palliata Station
summary: An Imperial task force led by Captain Lynae Caiton aboard the Broadsword, with the escorts Pillager and Prowler, attacks a New Republic Golan space battle station orbiting Coruscant to clear the way for ground landings. Imperial TIE squadrons (Black Squadron and War Shrike) engage New Republic Falcon, Ghost and Rogue fighters in a dogfight while the capital ships pound the station. The station is disabled and the mission is accomplished, but New Republic reinforcements led by the NRSD Fusillade and four Corellian gunships drop out of hyperspace and fire on the Broadsword. Caiton orders all units to fall back to the Malevolence rally point.

title: RPlog:Return Home
summary: Jessalyn Valios brings her partner and apprentice Orson Tighe to the abandoned Corellian archaeological colony on Yavin 4 where she was raised as an orphan. Walking through the overgrown ruins, she shares memories of her lonely childhood and love of the rainforest. They hike to a hidden spring-fed grotto full of flowers, strip down, and swim together, with Orson half-joking that they should live there, in a tender, intimate moment of respite.

title: RPlog:Return of a Jedi, Part I
summary: Simon Sezirok, disguised as the spacer 'Alvin Davis,' lands his coolant-leaking Firespray at Coronet Space Port on Corellia carrying the comatose Jessalyn Valios, whose soul is trapped in a Sith mind trap. Bazil McKenzie, escorting the ship with marines including Lehec, recognizes Simon by voice but lets him pass, while Simon's former student Mira Isaria, living as a beggar in the port, senses him and follows. Simon takes Jessalyn to the Lady of the Lake Hospital for life support, confronting Mira with bitter hatred over her abandonment of him and rejecting her apologies. He resolves to find a way to awaken Jessalyn using something of the Sith and tells Mira to do something real rather than apologize.

title: RPlog:Return of a Jedi, Part II
summary: Mira brings Simon a dark Sith puzzle box she stole from Luke Skywalker's belongings to help awaken the sleeping Jessalyn. Simon takes the troupe, including the entranced marine Lehec, to the Gold Beaches, where he performs a dark ritual, manipulating the box to siphon the life force of Mira, Lehec, and the surfer Orson Tighe who wanders into the storm. The ritual culminates in the puzzle box cracking open and Simon collapsing, finally freeing Jessalyn Valios, who awakens confused, asking for her lightsaber and the children. Simon introduces himself as a Selas of Telgosse and offers to help the disoriented, frightened Jedi to her feet.

title: RPlog:Return of the Prodigal Son
summary: Paul Nighman seeks treatment for a neck infection at a Mon Calamari hospital, where an angry Jessalyn confronts him over abandoning the group and over the situation with the teenage Shenner; they argue but reconcile, and Paul agrees to stay and accompany her. Back at the barracks, Paul tends to Shenner's badly damaged hands, learns she intends to enlist as a New Republic soldier, and gives her a shell ocarina as a gift. General Beaubos 'Skeezix' Calhoun and Gabrielle return from an evening together, and Paul settles in, telling his mentor Molari Canto by holocom that he is here to stay.

title: RPlog:Return to Renaal IV
summary: Gren Delede, flying the Z-95 Headhunter Cyclopean Rage and leading hired Vigilance Security mercenaries with the New Republic emblem hastily painted on, raids a lightly defended Imperial convoy in the Renaal system. Tak Dagon in the RX4 patrol craft Diligence destroys the escorting Guardian-class light cruiser while Gren's fighters engage Flight Officer Liza Molokai's TIE Interceptor squadron. Both rented Z-96 Headhunters are lost, but Gren shoots down Liza's interceptor again, forcing her to eject for the second time. The raid is judged a failure as the convoy escapes largely intact, though the raiders bled the Imperials and avoided paying the dead mercenary pilots.

title: RPlog:Reunion
summary: Jessalyn Valios reaches out through the Force to Luke Skywalker, who senses her and travels to the Gold Beaches on Corellia to reunite with her after nearly two years apart. They embrace and discuss recent events, including Luke's dealings with Emperor Valak and his apprentices, the missing third Death Star, and Jessalyn's rescue by Simon Sezirok. Jessalyn confesses she has taken Orson Tighe as an apprentice, and Luke grants permission for her to continue his non-combative training while reserving lightsaber instruction for himself. They part with plans to meet again aboard the Chancellor.

title: RPlog:Reunion and Redirection
summary: At the Union Starport on Caspar, Simon Sezirok and Mira Isaria brush off a cold Senior Ambassador Dises Diaglo after another firebean attack and search the landing pads for Markus Lisardis's ship. Mira, unknowingly guided by her latent Force sensitivity, leads them to the White Ghost, where they reunite with Markus, who had been captured with Bazil McKenzie and released after the destruction of Sluis Van. Markus describes feeling the True Source and the screaming deaths at Sluis Van, then asks Simon to train him rather than join Skywalker's Jedi. Simon agrees to teach Markus and Mira as 'brothers and sisters' of the Selas, and begins demonstrating that Mira's bean-training trick is actually nascent Force ability.

title: RPlog:Reunion of Old Friends
summary: In Chalmun's Cantina in Mos Eisley, a bandaged Morganna Tazecks, recovering from a sandstorm and Tusken attack, is recognized by her old acquaintance the doctor Jindra Langri. They are joined by Turk Avid, who survived the desert with Morganna, while a mysterious armed swordsman named Jairen watches from the bar. A polite stranger (Jairen) needles Morganna about her appearance, prompting her to bite back her temper to avoid trouble with patrolling stormtroopers. Stormtroopers check Morganna's weapons permit, which is in order, before she leaves drunk for a late business meeting.

title: RPlog:Reunion with the Princess
summary: On the Gold Beaches of Corellia, Leia Organa Solo finds her husband Han brooding over having nearly died fighting Simon the day before, and spots Jessalyn Valios nearby. The three reunite warmly, discussing the recent battle, Jessalyn's rescue by Cort Stasus and Simon, and the threat of the new Death Star, with Leia cautioning about secrecy. C-3PO arrives with a summons from Luke, and the group gathers aboard the Millennium Falcon where the injured Luke reassures them. The scene closes with Jessalyn reluctantly agreeing to introduce Orson to Luke and slipping away, uneasy in the Jedi Master's presence.

title: RPlog:Revealing Secrets
summary: Posing as a silver-haired woman named Annalise, Jessalyn Valios comes up short on credits while picking up a to-go order at the Sandbar in Plaxton City on Caspar, drawing the attention of the Corellian Kyyel Marrak, who covers her tab. Wary of the marine Tarrin present, she flees but later lures Kyyel out to Fountain Square and the Pula tree grove to thank and apologize to him, explaining she cannot accept help because she cannot trust herself. When Kyyel recites the Jedi creed his mother taught him, 'There is no emotion, there is only peace,' Jessalyn recognizes him as Force-sensitive, reveals her true name, admits she was once a Jedi, and flees in distress, leaving him with questions.

title: RPlog:Revelations to Antoine Dareus
summary: Grand Admiral Danik Kreldin, presumed dead and disguised as the old man 'Alois'/'Sergeant Malvis', travels to Caspar to find Imperial Ambassador Antoine Dareus at the Sandbar cantina. Amid drinks shared by Dareus, Kiare Quinn, Axel Vichten, and Asli Nayana, Kreldin maneuvers to isolate Dareus and reveals his true identity using a coded reference to their shared past on Ord Trasi. Dareus, overcome with loyalty, takes Kreldin to the secure Imperial Embassy office, kneels, and pledges his allegiance. Dareus commits to recruiting trusted commanders and bringing the 7th and 10th fleets to back Kreldin's treasonous revolution to overthrow the alien usurper Aleister Vadim and restore the Empire.

title: RPlog:Revenge
summary: Imperial Interceptor pilot Liza Molokai (callsign Slush) leads her squadron against VSC fighters raiding an Imperial supply depot on a forest moon, where she duels her recurring nemesis Gren Delede (callsign Blinky). The dogfight rages through the jungle canopy as both sides trade taunts and casualties. Liza finally shears off one of Gren's atmospheric wings, sending him into a dive, but the Marauder Eternal Vigilance tractor-beams him to safety. The VSC escape with their stolen cargo having lost two fighters; the Imperials lost three Interceptors, leaving the engagement a wash.

title: RPlog:Revenge in the Subway
summary: Danik Kreldin leads a disguised Imperial commando force (posing as 'CDC') into an underground Caspar train station to ambush and capture the Jedi Ezkhil i'Falzin during a blizzard. The troops evacuate civilians, shut down the tram power, and assault with flashbangs and tear gas, but Ezkhil cuts through Alpha team with his lightsaber and uses the Force to scatter the troopers. Kreldin charges Ezkhil with a combat knife and grapples him hand-to-hand, but the Jedi unleashes a wave of agonizing Force pain that knocks Kreldin unconscious. Ezkhil escapes the station, and Hellion Kracken orders the battered Imperials to grab the bodies and fall back, marking another failed operation.

title: RPlog:Revisiting Colonel Virda
summary: During the Corellian Corruption Scandal, ISB Director Korynn Fleming and the Sith Inquisitor Darth Malign (Tyler Damion) jointly interrogate CorSec Colonel Twila Virda in her Coronet City office under Imperial martial law. Malign uses Force telepathy to read Twila's thoughts while Korynn probes her about Governor Laran's ties to crime lord Tyy'sun and the Maffi syndicate; Malign declares her a liar and orders her arrest. Twila defiantly refuses to talk, and Korynn implants a Torian bloodworm into her arm to coerce her, ordering her taken away for interrogation while her ship is used as bait to lure her associates.

title: RPlog:Revolutions End
summary: In the climactic fleet battle for control of Nar Shaddaa, Darth Malign's New Sith Order and Imperial forces, anchored by the I2SD Predator and an Interdictor, crush the Hutt mercenary armada while sealing the moon with gravity wells. A combined New Republic (Audacity, Ghost Squadron, Luke Skywalker) and Corporate Sector Authority (Gren Delede's Authority) force intervenes to evacuate civilians and slaves from the moon, dueling the Sith fleet around the heavily damaged Interdictor Noquella's Embrace. The Embrace barely escapes to hyperspace toward Caspar, the New Republic and CSA forces extract their people and jump out, and Malign claims victory and orders the fleet to set course for the Bothan Federation.

title: RPlog:Right Place But Wrong Time
summary: In the lawless under-levels of Coruscant, Sith Senator Viktor Faust and his disguised aide Phair Kreiss (the alias of Jal'Dana Rall) discuss the dangers of their covert mission and Phair's future. Meanwhile, Sandor Woden, coerced by a blackmail note threatening his beloved Ai'kani, has been contracted to assassinate Faust with a sniper rifle. When Phair steps in front of Faust to tease him, she takes the shot meant for the Senator squarely in the back; she survives but is gravely wounded, and Sandor flees into the sewers in horror.

title: RPlog:Right of Return
summary: On Empire Day in space above blockaded Caspar, the cruiseliner Angry Rancor hails the Star Destroyer Nemesis seeking to repatriate 35 stranded Casparian passengers. Lt. Cmdr Amaza Sunrider negotiates terms with Captain Bruno, agreeing the Empire will receive, inspect, and deliver the passengers and provide supplies due to a humanitarian crisis. Lord Aldus Thel then privately instructs Sunrider to stage the arrest of a lone male passenger by planting incriminating articles, so the Empire can release him to Caspian custody as a propaganda gesture of goodwill.

title: RPlog:Rise of the Lizard King
summary: Lord Aldus Thel and the Emperor's chief agent Darth Venger (Vassily Korolov) land on Trandosha near the ruined village of Forak to convene the Trandoshan clan leaders, only to be ambushed by a band of clanless Trandoshan warriors at a booby-trapped meeting hall. After a fierce firefight in which Thel is badly wounded by an explosion and Venger cuts down the attackers, the two press on to the ruined New Sith temple where the clan elders are gathered. Thel declares the elders complicit in treachery and has Venger Force-choke all but one to death, installing the lone survivor, Kressk Bloodclaw, as a puppet 'Lizard King' to rule a unified, Empire-controlled Trandosha.

title: RPlog:Ritual of Darkness
summary: On Yavin 4, the fallen Selas Simon Sezirok and his blinded brother Markus Lisardis attempt a dark ritual requiring the blood of a Jedi, holding the captive padawan Jarin atop a Massassi temple. Jarin refuses to renounce the Jedi, so Simon ascends the ziggurat while Markus and the boy Lucky try to hold off the arriving Jedi (Jessalyn Valios, Mira Isaria, Kyokusha Gackt, Harlock, and later Aurejin). Simon kills the Jedi Harlock in a duel, then sacrifices Jarin with a dagger and Morganna's heart to summon a column of black fire meant to sever the Jedi from the Force. Jessalyn Valios sacrifices herself by dragging Simon into the dark fire, ending the ritual and leaving most participants horribly burned, Force-blinded, or dead; the Sith lord Bacharan Valak arrives at the climax and squares off against the blinded Aurejin.

title: RPlog:Room Service
summary: Aboard the Imperial Star Destroyer Nemesis, Stormtrooper officer Sivytra Nona brings a meal to the detained Ambassador Ambrosia Delgard and her unconscious cellmate Lt. Kovani. Sivytra inspects Ambrosia's ruined, partly cybernetic leg out of concern for infection and offers medical attention. Ambrosia, bitter and broken, reveals that Kovani is secretly her daughter, fathered by an adulterous moff, and that Duke Thel used a DNA match to extract her confession.

title: RPlog:Rules and Regulations
summary: Aboard the shuttle Darklighter on Tatooine, Paul Nighman shares a research lead from Dr. Canto about a dead Mandalorian historian's notes pointing to a hidden crypt containing Force-amplifier artifacts. Luke Skywalker arrives and reports that the bounty hunter Grathix, who heads the V'ez Tcha hunter group, may be holding a captive Corellian (possibly Han Solo); the group plans to meet Grathix openly at the Twin Suns Hotel. After Luke leaves, Paul reveals he must first detour to Corellia to retrieve the key artifact, and spends quiet time gifting Shenner clothes and a medkit, their interaction growing increasingly tender.

title: RPlog:Rumours and old friends
summary: On Liberation Day at the New Alderaan training grounds on Ord Mantell, Dr. Lynae Cassius meets Captain Rasi Cen at a quiet cafe to warn him about a suspicious job offer she received to weaponize an unstable pathogen, possibly tied to a Kreldin-style new Imperial Moff. Rasi pledges to alert intelligence contacts and departs. NRI Commander Galin, overhearing the word 'bioweapon' and choking on his caf, then introduces himself and arranges a formal meeting with Cassius, while the stim-addled, paranoid Drax Rendolen lurks outside the cafe window.

title: RPlog:Runther Interviews Piper
summary: At the Palanhi hotel, Alexis tells Piper she was confronted and threatened by a red-haired man who mistook her for someone named 'Shanna' working for 'Rogers,' twisting her injured arm; Piper offers her the protection of her Espo guards. CSA Auditor General Runther DeMagna then formally interviews Piper about her recent kidnapping by Sabbathius Osbourne, asking her to recount events from capture to release. Piper bristles at his arrogant manner and they exchange pointed words, after which Piper realizes Alexis is dangerously drowsy from mixing alcohol with painkillers and rushes to get food into her.

title: RPlog:S.O.S.
summary: Held captive aboard the ISD Nemesis in the Caspar system, Ambassador Ambrosia Delgard broadcasts a distress call on a New Republic military frequency. Ghost Squadron pilots Galin (Ghost 3, 'Wraith') and Captain Lance Corbet ('Wildman') in A-wings receive her clear signal and pinpoint her location on Deck Three, stateroom two, along with Lt Kovani. With four Imperial bogies closing in, the pilots promise to return and evacuate her within the week before Ambrosia's transmission is cut off as guards summon her.

title: RPlog:SW1 Log 1999-10-31-1
summary: A brief slice-of-life scene in the Ship Services area of the NRSC Deliverance where the Quarren technician Kelvis collects his pay and chats with the pilot Bazil McKenzie. They make small talk about the news, politics, and Bazil's assignment to Ghost Squadron flying X-wings before Kelvis heads off to sleep before his next shift.

title: RPlog:SW1 Log 1999-10-31-2
summary: In a hangar bay of the NRSC Deliverance, the diplomat Poguala, pilots Altair and Skeezix discuss the aging shuttle Tydirium and whether its recent modifications by the mechanic Tarroc are sound. Lianna returns from fixing an engineering mishap caused by a panicked technician, and Bazil wanders in. The scene is light banter among New Republic personnel about ship maintenance, capped by an in-game roster listing of the New Republic 'Who'.

title: RPlog:SW1 Log 1999-11-02
summary: A very short scene aboard the NRSC Deliverance in which the pilot Altair climbs out of her X-wing after a flight, and Gabrielle asks a deck officer about scheduled flights for a personal matter, deciding to take the recently repaired shuttle Tydirium out for a spin. Little of consequence occurs.

title: RPlog:SW1 Log 1999-12-06
summary: In a busy hangar bay aboard a New Republic vessel still recovering from a recent attack, X-wing pilot Bazil McKenzie lands and bickers with technicians over his damaged fighter. He meets Diplomatic Corps Representative Poguala Waaris-Dawntreader, who gently corrects his colorful language and notes his recent promotion. She thanks him for defending the fleet, checks on how he is coping with the deaths from the battle, and bids him to rest before he takes his leave.

title: RPlog:SW1 Log 1999-12-08-1
summary: At the Sluis Van landing field, pilot Bazil McKenzie loads cargo onto his shuttle Intrepid bound for Coruscant. A distracted woman named Ambry bumps into him and asks for a ride to Coruscant, which he grants. A red-headed newcomer, Irascius Quirn, asks how to reach StarOps to enlist; Bazil suggests he look around the base or the bar and wait until later. Names are exchanged before Bazil departs.

title: RPlog:SW1 Log 1999-12-09
summary: In a New Republic bar filling with military personnel after a battle, Commander Josh Arands sizes up the newcomer Irascius Quirn, who hopes to fly snubfighters. Quirn admits limited combat experience flying Toscan 8-Qs on his uncle's starbase. Arands offers him a chance to enlist, telling him to bring identity paperwork and meet on the flight deck the next day for a simulator test. Bazil and Nurse Gwen trade banter about pilots before Bazil departs to rest.

title: RPlog:SW1 Log 1999-12-11
summary: In a hangar bay, the Wookiee Geroam and the technician Lianna discuss taking a short break to attend races on Corellia. Geroam spots pilot Bazil McKenzie waiting by the shuttle Intrepid and asks if he is heading to Corellia. Bazil confirms he is bound for Corellia and offers the pair a ride, and they prepare to depart together.

title: RPlog:SW1 Log 1999-12-21
summary: Bazil McKenzie lands a shuttle at a crowded spaceport and is approached by an unknown civilian who hands him a note; Bazil reluctantly surrenders his bag, keeping a blaster carbine. He stares at the Caspian (CDU) Marine Corps dress uniform of Webb, who notices the scrutiny and asks if he can help. Bazil awkwardly excuses himself, then slips away in an unmarked shuttle-taxi to the lower city, secretly smirking, hinting at covert business.

title: RPlog:SW1 Log 1999-12-22
summary: In a VIP lounge, Representative Poguala Waaris-Dawntreader interviews Bazil McKenzie for a pilot post in the Diplomatic Corps and probes his violent past, revealing she knows he killed his slave-owner father to escape and free his enslaved mother. She harshly judges him a thug and murderer justified by his X-wing, walking him through non-violent ways he could have freed his mother. Ultimately she offers him a chance to heal: he must join the Corps under a violence-last clause, take etiquette, language, and cultural-sensitivity courses, and meet her tailor. Bazil, shaken but moved, accepts.

title: RPlog:SW1 Log 2000-01-05
summary: At Coruscant's spaceport, Representative Poguala summons Jedi Master Luke Skywalker to her shuttle to discuss a theft from the former Imperial Palace. She explains that among the stolen artifacts were her grandmother's journals and Palpatine's papers, hinting at a cloning facility possibly linked to a genocide on her homeworld Daavrix. Luke agrees to accompany the investigation, learns Han Solo is missing, and asks to meet with General D'agor; Bazil McKenzie works on the shuttle and is introduced as Poguala's pilot trainee. Throughout, Poguala is inexplicably and uncharacteristically unsettled in Luke's presence.

title: RPlog:SW1 Log 2000-01-11
summary: In the OREO board meeting room high above Corellia's capital, the ambitious Corellian John Donivan summons Bazil McKenzie over cognac and probes his dissatisfaction with his New Republic Diplomatic Corps career. After extracting Bazil's admission that he craves wealth and the independent life, Donivan recruits him as an informant, offering generous compensation for restricted information gleaned from his diplomatic position. Bazil, using his diplomatic training to mask his interest, agrees to keep their dealings secret but notes he has yet to encounter anything significant.

title: RPlog:SW1 Log 2000-01-15
summary: In the mess of a New Republic ship, Representative Poguala reports to Luke Skywalker that a background check on a woman calling herself Zenani Abotu found no record of such parentage on Tanaab, exposing her cover story. Jasmine recounts Zenani's mind-reading and cruelty toward Altair Quila on Gastus, and Poguala concludes Zenani is the former Imperial diplomat Morganna Tazecks. They agree to plan a legal capture, possibly luring her out using an adversary, and assign Bazil McKenzie to contact Altair Quila to arrange a meeting.

title: RPlog:SW1 Log 2000-01-16
summary: Bazil McKenzie wakes the sleeping Representative Poguala aboard a docked ship to report he has contacted Lieutenant Altair Quila, who was on patrol and has not yet responded. The conversation turns to Bazil's curiosity about the Jedi and Sith, and Poguala explains the Force is a genetic, ancient trait and that Force users operate on a separate plane, often hiding from one another. Bazil asks when he might take his pending diplomatic exams, and Poguala offers to administer the test on the spot.

title: RPlog:SW1 Log 2000-01-19
summary: In a New Republic hangar bay, Bazil McKenzie lands the newly received freighter NRS Internuncio and turns it over to technicians for inspection. Support Ops sergeant Lianna Blackledge, who has sneaked down to the hangar despite being told to rest, catalogs arriving crates and logs the Internuncio's first diagnostic work order. She tells Bazil the repulsorlift system needs realignment and estimates two to three hours of work before he heads off to the mess.

title: RPlog:SW1 Log 2000-01-19-2
summary: At the Imperial City spaceport on Coruscant, Devon Striker and Moira wait beside their ship Relentless, frustrated by the New Republic's cold reception while seeking a powerful Force-using woman. Nearby, Bazil McKenzie angrily works comlinks trying to get a message to Luke Skywalker on behalf of Representative Dawntreador. Moira psychically senses and pinpoints Bazil from across the spaceport as he repeatedly speaks Skywalker's name, startling Devon who had been searching for exactly that connection.

title: RPlog:SW1 Log 2000-01-24
summary: In a statue-filled square on Coruscant, Bazil McKenzie finds Altair Quila and tells her he has transferred out of the diplomatic corps, finding it too uptight. He asks to rejoin Ghost Squadron, and Altair, now its CO with a vacant XO slot, offers him the executive officer position, which he accepts. They reminisce about Star Ops and the still-missing Andael, whose spot Altair had been keeping open.

title: RPlog:SW1 Log 2000-01-30
summary: Aboard the NRSC Deliverance's auxiliary bay, Altair Quila argues with a stubborn Quartermaster while Roberto reviews efficiency reports and complains about supply-line paperwork holdups delaying spare parts for Wayland Squadron. Bazil McKenzie arrives late in civilian clothes, explaining he was detained by Corellian Customs as a suspected Imperial spy and had trouble locating the distant warship. The trio banter about which ship Bazil will fly, joking about a smelly garbage scow before he settles on an X-wing as Ghost 5.

title: RPlog:SW1 Log 2000-02-05
summary: On the Ship Services deck of the NRSC Deliverance, the Wookiee officer Geroam confronts a sleepy or drunk Ensign (Wes) for disrespecting a senior officer. When the Ensign continues his defiance, Geroam hauls him off toward the brig. Bazil McKenzie arrives with fellow pilot Jenkins too late to intervene, exchanges greetings with Altair, and watches warily, scanning newcomers including the proud, authoritative John Donivan.

title: RPlog:SW1 Log 2000-02-09-S
summary: A New Republic task force led by the Mon Calamari cruiser Deliverance and a supporting gunship jumps into the Wistril system to seize Imperial supply caches, engaging the Imperial Victory-Class Star Destroyer Volatile and four squadrons of TIE fighters. Ghost (including Bazil as Ghost 5), Archer, Rogue, and Draco squadrons dogfight the TIEs while the capital ships trade heavy fire; Bazil loses wingmen and is repeatedly saved by Ghost 11. After fighters target a weakened starboard shield generator with torpedoes, the Volatile's engines are crippled and the Star Destroyer breaks apart, leaving the NR forces to claim the cargo containers.

title: RPlog:SW1 Log 2000-02-15
summary: Aboard the New Republic cruiser NRSC Deliverance, Lieutenant Jasmine wakes mid-sleepwalk in her nightgown, being escorted by Commodore Dauthus (a Sullustan/Quarren officer) and Sergeant Kelvis. They are alarmed that her eyes turn purple and that she runs a fever during these episodes, suspecting something abnormal. Jasmine downplays it as stress-induced sleepwalking and is allowed to return to her quarters under security watch, escorted by Kelvis.

title: RPlog:SW1 Log 2000-05-17
summary: At the Lucky Krayt casino aboard a ship over a planet, Bazil McKenzie sits down at Lando Calrissian's high-stakes sabacc table after winning a 30,000-credit lottery prize. Putting on a calculated gambler's facade learned on Tatooine, Bazil wins the first hand with a Pure Sabacc, taking 4,000 credits from Lando. On the second hand he loses to Lando's score of 18. Lianna and Mysti briefly observe and pass through the casino.

title: RPlog:SW1 Log 2000-05-19
summary: After being knocked out by the Sith Morganna during an interrogation attempt in the brig, Bazil McKenzie wakes in the medbay with a concussion. He argues loudly with a nurse, demanding to be released to speak with Captain Darklighter (Howie), even panicking at the mention of possible brain damage. Commander Kierv, also injured in the same incident, lies in a nearby bed groaning, and both are confined to the medbay under orders.

title: RPlog:SW1 Log 2000-05-20
summary: Bazil McKenzie, having been ostensibly fired from his StarOps post (a cover story), arrives in handcuffs at New Republic Intelligence headquarters on Coruscant to assemble a covert team. He meets the Twi'lek Xanatos (alias Baldur) and the NRI woman Mersche/Kathryn, who spontaneously joins them. Adopting the alias Mege Vauki, Bazil leads the team aboard the YT-1300 Triton on a mission to find the new Death Star and steal its plans.

title: RPlog:SW1 Log 2000-05-21-1
summary: Aboard the Triton in an asteroid field near the destroyed planet Gastus, Mege (Bazil) and Mersche survey the debris of the world the new Death Star annihilated days earlier, finding nothing salvageable. Xanatos recovers from spacesickness and joins the discussion of the mission. The team debates how to proceed, deciding to contact non-Imperial intelligence agencies and allies like the Corporate Sector Authority and the Griffon Alliance, with Xanatos tasked to handle diplomatic outreach to a contact named Eva Sargent. They jump to Caspar.

title: RPlog:SW1 Log 2000-05-21-2
summary: Aboard the NRSC Deliverance, Captain Darklighter (Howie), NRI Captain Jonau Diggs, Luke Skywalker, and Midshipman Loraiil conduct a sedated interrogation of the captured Sith Moff Morganna Tazecks regarding the new Death Star. Luke restrains her mind while probing her memories, finding she knows only that the station exists and destroyed a planet, plus fragments about Valak, Jack Raven, and a Krayt Dragon. Morganna lunges and bites Luke's shoulder; guards stun her unconscious, ending a largely fruitless interrogation.

title: RPlog:SW1 Log 2000-05-22-1
summary: After several days establishing contacts and liaisons on Caspar, the team aboard the Triton (Vauki/Bazil, Mersche, Xanatos) grows frustrated and decides to leave. Xanatos reports that approaching the Corporate Sector Authority is unwise and recommends the friendlier Griffon Alliance instead. They clear Casperian space after a radio check with the CMS Typhoon and set course for Cochran, the Griffon homeworld, to seek a military man named Patricio Rey.

title: RPlog:SW1 Log 2000-05-22-2
summary: On Caspar at the New Republic Embassy in Plaxton City, NRI Captain Jonau Diggs seeks out diplomatic representative Eva Sargent. He encounters her Mon Calamari aide Lono, who is initially cautious and demands verification of Diggs's identity and purpose. After Diggs presents his NRI credentials and cites classification acts, Lono escorts him to Sargent's residence in the embassy housing wing, though the scene ends before they speak as Sargent is idle.

title: RPlog:SW1 Log 2000-05-22-3
summary: On leave after the New Republic pushed Imperial forces out of the Tatooine system, X-wing pilot Bazil McKenzie (Xeres) and his commander Howie Darklighter visit a quiet cantina in Bazil's desolate hometown of Brinkwater and share a rare bottle of pre-Endor Corellian whiskey. Bazil reveals his lost love Casey via a holo-locket, and Howie confides about his own vanished diplomat sweetheart, the two bonding over shared heartbreak and the notion of fate. They philosophize about loss as a defense mechanism and decide to stay at a local inn rather than risk flying back drunk, cementing a deep friendship.

title: RPlog:SW1 Log 2000-05-24-1
summary: NRI agent Mege Vauki arrives undercover at Pride-1 station hoping to meet Griffon general Patricio Rey to gather intelligence on the new Imperial Death Star, but the general is absent. A diplomatic woman, Thayra Delmund, vets him; after he proves his New Republic Intelligence credentials, she takes him to the Galaxy Rose tavern to talk. They exchange information about the Death Star that destroyed Gastus, confirming the Griffons know little more than the Republic, and agree to share future intelligence before parting.

title: RPlog:SW1 Log 2000-05-24-2
summary: Leaving his meeting with Thayra, the tired NRI agent Mege Vauki re-encounters the cargo hauler Khirsah in the Pride-1 hangar bay, the man he had briefly crossed paths with over a holoterminal. The two make casual small talk about cargo runs, profitable trade routes, and swoop racing. Recognizing Vauki's exhaustion, Khirsah cuts the conversation short and they part amicably, both sensing they will meet again.

title: RPlog:SW1 Log 2000-05-24-3
summary: NRI agent Mege Vauki lands the Triton at the Imperial City spaceport on Coruscant to pick up a technician for his Death Star mission. A friendly young man, Marcus (Marc Delvennis), offers him coffee and then reveals he is the very tech Vauki was sent to collect. After confirming identities, Vauki briefs Marcus that they are heading into Imperial space to find and slice the Death Star's plans, and brings him aboard.

title: RPlog:SW1 Log 2000-05-25-1
summary: Aboard the NRSC Deliverance, Captain Howie Darklighter and Intel liaison Captain Jonau Diggs convene an emergency briefing revealing the next Death Star target is the peaceful Ithorian homeworld of Ithor. Senior figures including Luke Skywalker, Lando Calrissian, General Arands, diplomat Eva Sargent and Admiral Dauthus debate strategy. They prioritize quietly evacuating Ithor (transports already dispatched) and weigh risky options including a covert sabotage insertion, an Interdictor ambush, makeshift drilling torpedoes, and a strike-force rampage through Imperial systems. The log ends mid-discussion before a final plan is set.

title: RPlog:SW1 Log 2000-05-25-2
summary: Mege Vauki's NRI team flies the Triton deep into Imperial space to the capital world Dreven, encountering a massive Imperial fleet of thousands of ships. Forced to land at Admiral Bevven Spaceport, they are met by Imperial Commander Zeak (Oppenhiemer) and stormtroopers who ground and impound their ship under a 'health and safety inspection' that is clearly a thorough teardown. The team maintains cover identities (Vauki as a swooper, Marcus as his brother, Mersche as his girlfriend/singer, Xanatos as his slave), but the Empire's actions suggest someone has flagged them.

title: RPlog:SW1 Log 2000-05-30-1
summary: After a few hours on the Imperial capital Dreven, Mege Vauki's team is rounded up and escorted to the spaceport, where an agitated Commander Zeak orders them to leave the system immediately. Zeak, frustrated after a reprimand from his superiors, returns their repaired ship and cargo intact but bars them from ever returning. Vauki feigns gratitude, and the Triton departs under TIE Interceptor escort, jumping to hyperspace bound for Tatooine, the team having gained little intel beyond knowledge of the huge fleet.

title: RPlog:SW1 Log 2000-05-30-2
summary: Back on Tatooine in Mos Eisley, NRI agent Mege Vauki again runs into the cargo hauler Khirsah, marking their third chance encounter in a week. After shooing off harassing Jawas, the two retire to Chalmun's Cantina with Vauki's Twi'lek crewmate Xanatos, sharing a bottle of pre-Endor Corellian whiskey. Khirsah peppers Vauki with questions about swoop racing while the men bond over their connoisseurship of fine, increasingly rare aged liquor before Khirsah departs with a glass for the road.

title: RPlog:SW1 Log 2000-06-03
summary: In Fountain Square in Plaxton City on Caspar, Sar Admiral Salonika Batrad violently assaults IGNews reporter Loy Marin over a story alleging that New Republic General Joshua Arands fathered Salonika's child. Marin pleads he only read the story rather than wrote it, while Arands intervenes and faces off against Salonika, even tossing his gunbelt aside and daring the Admiral to go through him. Salonika ultimately releases the battered Marin without striking the final blow, and businessman Ben Jawatha helps the reporter up and offers him sanctuary at Enterprise Towers. The confrontation nearly sparks a Caspar-New Republic incident as armed marines and NRI agents converge.

title: RPlog:SW1 Log 2000-06-04-1
summary: Reporter Loy Marin returns to Fountain Square in Plaxton City intending to wrap up his Caspar senate story and summons his camera droid. While waiting, he conducts a brief interview with the melancholy spacer Mege Vauki (Bazil McKenzie undercover), asking about Caspar's political situation and greatest strength; Vauki, a Tatooine-born trader, says Caspar's self-sufficiency and ability to stay out of other factions' affairs is its strength. Vauki nervously dislikes the camera and gives only short answers before drifting off in sad reflection over a lost love named Casey. Marin decides to hold off on concluding his senate story another day.

title: RPlog:SW1 Log 2000-06-08
summary: Aboard the New Republic ship NRSC Deliverance, NRI Captain Jonau Diggs encounters Jedi Master Luke Skywalker in the command corridor. They discuss the stalled intelligence efforts, including reconfirmation of the Death Star's location and the escape of Moff Tazecks (Morganna) from Caspian custody. Luke asks Diggs to look into the Moff's matters, and Diggs agrees, since his intelligence agents could use the change of focus. Luke offers his help if needed and the two part cordially.

title: RPlog:SW1 Log 2000-06-09-1
summary: At the Reckless Abandon Swoop Arena's staging area in Coronet City, Corellia, swoop racer Mege Vauki (Bazil McKenzie undercover) grumbles about waiting for swoops to arrive for practice. He meets a curious newcomer, the Meditersarian Luther Syss, and guides him from the staging area through the main concourse toward the viewing stands. The two introduce themselves and shake hands, with Vauki noting he races Grade One for Team KIF, before parting ways amiably.

title: RPlog:SW1 Log 2000-06-09-2
summary: A swoop race at the Reckless Abandon Swoop Arena on Corellia pits Joshua Arands on the Event Horizon (Grade 4, Team Corellia) against Team KIF racers Kestryan du Bracc on the Repossessor (Grade 2) and Mege Vauki on the LoanShark (Grade 1). The racers navigate a multi-environment track including Coronet beaches, Kashyyyk forests, Tibannopolis, coral reefs, a Lava Dragon habitat, Beggar's Canyon, Hoth glaciers with a live Wampa, and the Anoat sewers with a Dianoga. Arands finishes first overall with a disappointing 567 SRU pace time, Kestryan sets 321 for Grade 2 after braving the Dianoga, and rookie Vauki sets a surprising 199 SRU Grade 1 pace.

title: RPlog:SW1 Log 2000-06-10-1
summary: At the Reckless Abandon Swoop Arena, Mege Vauki (Bazil McKenzie undercover) joins Luther Syss in the stands to watch a swoop race featuring Altair Quila ('Nature Girl') on the Achernar against the Pallando siblings Ernie and Whitney of Team Caspar. Vauki and Luther loudly cheer Altair on and boo Ernie, who ultimately beats Vauki's score. The race ends in spectacular crashes for both Pallandos at the Dianoga, while Eva Sargent watches with concern before heading off to find Caspian team crew. Final scores: Whitney 332, Ernie 276, Altair 178 SRU.

title: RPlog:SW1 Log 2000-06-10-2
summary: In the staging area of the Reckless Abandon Swoop Arena, Bazil McKenzie (undercover as swoop racer Mege Vauki) encounters injured Ghost Squadron pilot Colonel Altair Quila and the civilian Eva Sargent after Altair's race. Bazil compliments his former Ghost Squadron comrade and introduces himself as Mege Vauki of Team KIF, giving no sign of recognition. Altair suddenly recognizes him and blurts 'Baz!', hastily covering it with a fake cough; Bazil deflects with a story about disease-carrying Jawas and subtly signals her to keep silent. He confirms his identity vaguely while preserving his cover and departs.

title: RPlog:SW1 Log 2000-06-10-3
summary: In the stands of the Reckless Abandon Swoop Arena, the disguised Bazil McKenzie (Vauki) lingers while Eva Sargent, Colonel Altair Quila, and a retired soldier named Halloway watch another swoop race. Halloway, whose regiment was disbanded after the Battle of Endor, chats with Altair about military life and the New Republic's scattered forces, lending her a handkerchief to wipe her injured foot. The race plays out across the arena's hazardous sections, with Simone on the Terminal Velocity narrowly losing first to McTavish and Howie on the Firedancer taking second in Grade 2. Kestryan watches Kartijan cross the finish line, unsure whether to be pleased by her victory.

title: RPlog:SW1 Log 2000-06-10-4
summary: After a grueling swoop race at the Reckless Abandon Arena on Corellia, FLS racer Kartijan Lore and FLS security officer Tarrin are treated for injuries while KIF rookie racer Mege Vauki approaches to congratulate them. Vauki reports that Kartijan's strong run bumped him from second to third place and recounts the weekend's casualties, including racers killed by the Dianago and Wampa course hazards. Nursing a back injury, Kartijan invites Vauki to the FLS stables to chat, and the scene wraps as the players agree out-of-character to idle conversation about swoop racing.

title: RPlog:SW1 Log 2000-06-10-5
summary: Swoop racer and freighter captain Mege Vauki returns to the Coronet spaceport where Marcus is performing maintenance atop the Triton, and the two head to the Smuggler's Dig cantina for Corellian whiskey. Over drinks, Vauki opens up about a woman named Casey Johnson who vanished about a year ago after talking of joining the Republic, despite his three months of searching and hiring a slicer. Marcus, who has access to better channels than a local slicer, offers to investigate her disappearance, and the two toast the Republic before turning in for the night.

title: RPlog:SW1 Log 2000-06-11-1
summary: At the Coronet spaceport, businesswoman Shannon disembarks from a CEC shuttle eager to catch the swoop races and strikes up a brief conversation with Mege Vauki as he rolls his swoop off his freighter. Vauki explains he is a first-time grade-one racer currently in third place, with a ship and twenty-five thousand credits as the division's first prize. Shannon wishes him luck, he introduces himself as Mege Vauki of Team KIF, and he rides off into the spaceport traffic.

title: RPlog:SW1 Log 2000-06-12-1
summary: In Coronet City's Nebula Plaza late at night, Mege Vauki finds the elderly Wookiee Chongatakiee furiously assaulting a malfunctioning Galactic Lotto machine that stole his credits. Vauki offers to help, opens the machine, and discovers and fixes a loose power connection, producing the Wookiee's ticket. The two introduce themselves; Chongatakiee, a scarred former captive now working as an Alliance technician, thanks Vauki and offers his aid, and the pair part on warm terms.

title: RPlog:SW1 Log 2000-06-12-2
summary: At the Coronet spaceport, the Imperial-aligned Sarian commodore Pallando sneaks up on the elderly New Republic intelligence officer Captain Jonau Diggs, who is reading on a bench. The two trade terse, sardonic words, with Pallando feeling out the man he is about to be paired with on an intelligence mission and Diggs offering only select, jaded replies. They agree to meet at the embassy in two days, and Diggs privately reflects on his decades of thankless service and his distaste for the upcoming assignment.

title: RPlog:SW1 Log 2000-06-15-1
summary: Aboard the NRSC Deliverance, pilot Bazil McKenzie returns from a month away after being cleared of charges of plotting to kill a prisoner, fighting with a malfunctioning pay machine while greeting Warrant Officer Kelvis Aquatica and new Ghost Squadron trainee Vek Eldar. Pilot Kierv joins as the group discusses the Imperial Death Star that destroyed Gastus and debates Imperial strategy and how to attack it. Bazil, secretly an intelligence operative, hints he learned things on Coruscant, then heads off to find Colonel Quila, learning she has again damaged a Ghost Squadron ship.

title: RPlog:SW1 Log 2000-06-16
summary: Colonel Altair Quila recognizes her former XO Bazil McKenzie in the Deliverance's hangar bay, and he invites her to lunch at the upscale White Knight nightclub on Coruscant. Over an expensive meal Bazil quietly reveals he was never imprisoned but has been working a covert Imperial-focused intelligence assignment, while Altair recounts how Sith conditioned her during Imperial custody to react to certain trigger words. Bazil grows quietly attracted to her, and after lunch the pair take a hovertaxi and air tram tour of Coruscant, during which Bazil shares his painful past as a Tatooine slave who killed his father.

title: RPlog:SW1 Log 2000-06-19
summary: At a Coruscant military starport, Bazil McKenzie shows off his newly purchased microfreighter Dawntreader to his former captain Howie, who reveals he has impulsively proposed to Jasmine Heretin and needs help choosing an engagement ring. Bazil agrees to be best man and confides that his Intel charges over the moff Morganna are resolved, that he was found not guilty, and that he was promoted to Lieutenant Commander. Howie teases out Bazil's crush on Colonel Altair Quila and offers to discreetly find out her feelings as the two head to a jewelry store, which they enter just as the scene ends.

title: RPlog:SW1 Log 2000-06-20
summary: In the auxiliary hangar bay of the New Republic carrier NRSC Deliverance, Colonel Altair Quila assigns the green cadet Vek his first X-wing, Ghost 11, while grounding him from solo flight until his injured hand heals. Warrant Officer Kelvis confronts Altair about a ventricle stabilizer she broke while tinkering with Ghost One, prompting her to promise to ask the techs for help in future. Bazil McKenzie lingers nervously near Altair before excusing himself to run errands on Coruscant, and Wedge Antilles stops by to collect Rogue Squadron status reports and point out the Colonel's upside-down rank pins.

title: RPlog:SW1 Log 2000-06-22-1
summary: Colonel Altair Quila strides into the Deliverance hangar bay calling for a dogfight, and Bazil McKenzie surprises everyone by arriving in full uniform, having returned to duty early. Wedge Antilles and the cadets Vek and Avitar volunteer, proposing two-on-two teams. Lt. Lianna agrees to tune down the fighter capacitors for a more realistic but low-damage training exercise.

title: RPlog:SW1 Log 2000-06-22-2
summary: The New Republic pilots launch from the Deliverance over Coruscant for a training dogfight, with Altair (Ghost One), Wedge (Rogue One), Vek (Ghost 11), Avitar (A-wing) and Bazil (Ghost 5) exchanging mock fire. Meanwhile in the hangar, Representative Eva Sargent gets Lt. Lianna Blackledge to diagnose a suspected hydraulic leak in the landing gear of her freighter Internuncio. The dogfight escalates with Altair firing a torpedo at her new recruit before relief fighters arrive; the scene ends OOC as the author had to leave.

title: RPlog:SW1 Log 2000-06-25
summary: In the Deliverance mess hall, General Arands dismisses an NRI shadow assigned to monitor him and relaxes with his XO Lelila and Archer pilots Avitar and Vek over drinks. Bazil McKenzie storms in, furious that NRI agents ransacked his quarters over the murder charge he was already acquitted of, downing a triple shot of Corellian Blood Vodka and stabbing his datapad with a vibro-knife. Vek bridges the two groups, chatting with Bazil about the search and then joining Arands' table, where talk turns to recruit shortages and a possible upcoming operation, before Bazil pays for the mess and stumbles off to bed.

title: RPlog:SW1 Log 2000-08-05
summary: A short slice-of-life scene in the Deliverance mess hall where the engaged couple Kelvis (a Quarren tech) and Lelila chat after Kelvis exasperatedly sends off his slow-learning subordinate tech Jinx to read the manual. Bazil McKenzie, now openly wearing an NRI/intel patch, wanders in looking exhausted, quietly orders a drink, and downs it before a comlink call summons him away again.

title: RPlog:SW1 Log 2000-08-07-1
summary: At the upscale White Knight VIP Nightclub, Bazil McKenzie (using the alias Vauki) meets with the Twi'lek operative Xanatos and a new recruit, Kimber Pallas, to hand off official release grants and mission authorization for an undercover operation aboard a luxury casino ship. Xanatos pitches the assignment to Kimber, describing the ship's amenities, signing bonus, and animal menagerie, while Bazil confirms his own shift from field work to administrative intelligence duty. Bazil departs for the mothership after confirming the secure encryptions remain unchanged.

title: RPlog:SW1 Log 2000-08-07-2
summary: In the NRI computer center at Military HQ, intelligence officer Bazil McKenzie meets veteran agent Kathryn Jrevelian (alias Mersche), who is collecting reports including one from the Pallando team on Caspar. The two trade news about Bazil's heavy administrative workload covering for Director Adian Ward, then Bazil quietly enlists Kathryn's help to gather intelligence on John Donivan, CEO of OREO Enterprises and owner of the Republic's bank system, whom he believes is dangerously corrupt and bribing high officials. Kathryn agrees to dig for dirt through her underworld contact Troy while she travels to Caspar.

title: RPlog:SW1 Log 2000-08-07-3
summary: On Caspar, NRI Chief Bazil McKenzie intercepts Representative Eva Sargent during her evening jog, trailed by her two bodyguards, to question her about John Donivan. He probes her on Donivan's influence as head of the First Bank and the likelihood that he bribes members of the military, Diplomatic Corps, and Senate. Sargent, reluctant to be drawn into a criminal investigation, lists possible charges (campaign-finance violations, conspiracy, tax crimes) before Bazil thanks her and vanishes into the shadows.

title: RPlog:SW1 Log 2000-08-07-4
summary: On Coruscant, New Republic pilots Lelila and Enb'Zik run into Bazil McKenzie, who leads them down into the dangerous lower levels of the city in search of a cantina. Ambushed by a Noghri, a Twi'lek, and a human thug, Enb'Zik bluffs a nonexistent 'Gamma Team' of snipers over his comlink while Bazil covers them with blasters, scaring off two attackers. Bazil's NRI quick-response transport, piloted by Forest, arrives to extract them, revealing him as New Republic Intelligence. The scene closes with talk of fleet tension and rumors of a third Death Star.

title: RPlog:SW1 Log 2000-08-08-1
summary: Bazil McKenzie, working undercover in civilian dress with another armed NRI agent in the plaza of the former Imperial Palace on Coruscant, waits to meet a new recruit. The Bothan recruit Jocal, a former Kessel spice smuggler from Corellia, arrives and bumps into Bazil. Bazil confirms Jocal is accepted into New Republic Intelligence, hands him a datapad with training information and instructions to check into an inn near the military complex. The log is explicitly noted as an OOC RP test to gauge the player Jocal's experience.

title: RPlog:SW1 Log 2000-08-08-2
summary: At a disguised New Republic Intelligence facility hidden beneath the sands of Tatooine's Dune Sea (posing as a moisture farm), Kathryn Montavre-Jrevelian gives Bazil McKenzie a tour. She shows off the facility's hidden defenses, including eight heavy quad-laser cannons, sixteen illegal Gladiator X5 assassin droids, a self-destruct system, fifteen comm uplinks, and a concealed bar. The two officers discuss the facility's strategic value as an entry point into Imperial space, then Kathryn turns in for the night while Bazil studies a star display zooming in on a distant asteroid.

title: RPlog:SW1 Log 2000-08-08-3
summary: In the Coruscant palace plaza, Bazil McKenzie meets Snal'Fashtalli ('Snarl'), the Horansi wife of pilot Enb'Zik, after the lower-city incident that endangered her husband. They discuss her unease in the military's rigid protocol, the prejudice her people and her cross-species marriage face, and her recovery from injuries. Bazil, revealing he heads New Republic Intelligence, offers Snarl and Enb'Zik freelance or staff work with his branch amid the rising Death Star threat. Snarl, claustrophobic on the metal city-world but interested, asks what sort of job he has in mind.

title: RPlog:SW1 Log 2000-08-09
summary: Bazil McKenzie meets Enb'Zik at a Coruscant nightclub; using coded talk about buying a ship, they move to the streets to speak privately about the NRI job offer. Enb'Zik offers to help monitor Imperial fleet movements and reveals his motivation: keeping his nearby homeworld Sullust safe from the Death Star. Bazil confides that the Death Star's next target is Ithor, already being evacuated, and that Imperial flagships are massed at Dreven and Selene. Enb'Zik agrees to work for Intelligence in exchange for an unmarked A-Wing and the chance to fly in the final assault on the Death Star.

title: RPlog:SW1 Log 2000-08-10
summary: Bazil McKenzie ambushes his former CO Colonel Altair Quila in her Ghost Squadron office aboard the Deliverance to ask a favor on behalf of NRI recruit Enb'Zik. He requests that Enb'Zik be allowed to fly in the final assault on the Death Star in an unmarked, used A-Wing he would pay for himself. Altair argues an unmarked spy ship is impractical for the battle and proposes Enb'Zik fly one of the squadron's standard fighters instead, pending General Arands' approval. They also briefly discuss the stalled effort to find a way to destroy the Death Star and the ongoing evacuation of Ithor.

title: RPlog:SW1 Log 2000-08-14
summary: In the Deliverance hangar bay, Lelila reunites with pilot Vek before Bazil McKenzie appears wrapped in a sheet and wielding a sword, having suffered a violent mental breakdown. Bazil cycles through delusional personalities, first believing himself a Jedi with Lelila as his apprentice 'Traxari' and Vek as a Sith, then an Imperial Commodore calling Vek 'Emanuel'. Vek draws his blaster and calls security as Bazil grows dangerous, until he collapses, mistaking Altair Quila for his lost love Casey and reliving imprisonment on Nar Shaddaa. A medical team arrives as Bazil, lucid for a moment, pleads for help while his mind fractures again.

title: RPlog:SW1 Log 2000-09-20
summary: In a Coruscant hotel room, the dark-sider Kacela (Morganna) renders Bazil McKenzie unconscious and probes his insanity-wracked mind for intelligence after seducing him at the White Night club. She glimpses fragmentary images of Selene, prominent figures like John Donivan and Ernie Pallando, and a mysterious CDU Marine woman named Casey Johnson, but the sheer ferocity of Bazil's insanity overwhelms her. Struggling, she implants a coercion to fuzz out her own identity, leaving Bazil thinking she was merely a friend who gave him tea. She flees disturbed, while Bazil's twisted mind whispers ominous farewells.

title: RPlog:SW1 Log 2001-01-05
summary: Aboard the New Republic Star Destroyer Chancellor, NRI director Bazil McKenzie surprises the off-duty crowd in the observation lounge by performing as the lead singer of his band, the Wassailers, a melancholy song about a lost home. Patrons including pilot Drax, officer Tane Wystler, the diplomat-like cyborg Lady Kathryn, the musician Lelila, and the harried mother Johanna with her son Etiel watch and react. Luke Skywalker, in a hooded meditation, briefly meets Tane before being called away by news that patient Eva Sargent's brainwaves are stabilizing. The performance ends to applause and the band packs up.

title: RPlog:SW1 Log 2001-01-09
summary: In a preserved Massassi amphitheater on Yavin, Bazil McKenzie summons Jasmine to reveal that his investigation into his own origins uncovered that they are biological siblings, separated when he was taken to the Tatooine slave mines 27 years earlier. He recounts being raised by the slave-colony owner he later killed and learning from his dying adoptive mother that his real family existed. Jasmine, overwhelmed by grief over her dead parents and lost brother Adian, breaks down and collapses, and Bazil comforts her. They reconcile to the new relationship and leave the cold ruins for his ship.

title: RPlog:SW1 Log 2001-04-06
summary: During a starship firefight, Bazil McKenzie (using the persona Grassius Knoll) revives the smuggler Karrde aboard the freighter Flippant Minion, with Wedge piloting. Bazil reveals he kidnapped Karrde on Luke Skywalker's orders to keep him from a deadly meeting with Admiral Cimber and to protect Karrde's secret ties to the New Republic. Pursued by Griffon and Covenant vessels demanding they halt, Karrde negotiates over comms with the Covenant leader Valen aboard the Masquerade, talking their way out. The Minion evades the ships and jumps to hyperspace.

title: RPlog:SW1 Log 2007-06-16
summary: Years after resigning from the New Republic, a now heavily cyborg-scarred Bazil McKenzie arrives in the Imperial-controlled Nelona system aboard his assault shuttle, the Dirty Jawa, unexpectedly finding an Imperial fleet. The Star Destroyer Inquisitor orders him to halt and submit to boarding. Bazil cooperates, presenting his papers and explaining he wishes to reclaim property he owned in the Menrai district years earlier. The Imperial lieutenant notes his checkered NRI/Inner Council background but clears him to descend to the planet under lockdown pending a background check.

title: RPlog:SW1 Log spencer.rptest
summary: This page is not a role-play log. It contains only a wiki redirect pointing to the page 'RPlog:Spencer RP Test'. There is no scene content, characters, or events.

title: RPlog:Sabacc Tournament
summary: At the first weekly Sabacc tournament in the House of Sabacc on Nar Shaddaa, gamblers gather including the exiled Imperial Antoine Dareus, Twi'lek Tal'sin Secura, casino MC Ewan Dain, Jedi Johanna Siri te Danaan, and Ortolan musician Frederiko Eembek. Before the tournament, Dina (Johanna's alias) violently slaps and ejects Dain's date in a catfight while Dareus wins a side bet on the outcome. In the heads-up tournament finale, Tal'sin outplays Dareus across multiple hands to win the 10,000-credit grand prize, unaware that Dareus had previously shot him unconscious during a prior mission.

title: RPlog:Sabrina's Capture by Boba Fett
summary: Bounty hunter Boba Fett ambushes Jedi padawan Sabrina Rheatis in Coruscant's Parliamentary District as part of an Imperial-bounty campaign to capture Luke Skywalker's new Jedi. Sabrina triggers building alarms, wrestles Fett's rifle away, and flees, frantically calling out through the Force to Luke for help that never comes. After a running fight involving flamethrower, grappling cord, and a rocket fired at the responding guards, Fett tackles her, slams her head against the permacrete, and sedates her with the same Force-suppressing poison used on captured Jedi Brandis and Ai'kani, carrying her off as his third Jedi prisoner.

title: RPlog:Salt Shaker
summary: On the Drev'starn spaceport on Bothawui, the anti-Force zealot Simon Sezirok arrives seeking to negotiate the release of the kidnapped Sarian clan leader Ubi Sunt, encountering Jedi Knight Jessalyn Valios, bounty hunters Racra and Ayra, and the Bothan operative Krinko. While Simon meets briefly with Grand Protector Cernious Vry'lya, the Sarian terrorist Tarazed Brum and her mercenaries (Dibar, Dar'siden) plant chlorine-gas charges among salt containers as revenge against the Bothans. The bombs detonate in waves, releasing poison gas and fire that kill thousands; Jessalyn kills Tarazed with her lightsaber, Luke Skywalker arrives but hesitates to strike Simon, and Krinko's heirloom sword is shattered by Simon's Force-empowered staff. Simon and his young ward Lucky escape into the chaos as the spaceport is devastated.

title: RPlog:Saving Grace
summary: After five days confined alone in Ullo's palace on Tatooine, a despondent, drinking Ylsa is roused by her servant Ilyana, who secretly plays a familiar song and passes her a shell as proof of a message. Ilyana reveals she has been secretly helping Paul Nighman, who survived the ship attack hidden in a storage hatch and approached her in Mos Eisley begging for help. Ylsa, overcome with emotion, learns Paul is devising an escape plan and loves her; she sends back a message to be patient and avoid risk, then shares a meal with Ilyana, treating the servant girl as an equal.

title: RPlog:Scaven, the Merciless
summary: In an abandoned Nar Shaddaa warehouse, Moff Scaven Marx finalizes a deal with Paris Sieara, CEO of Reyon Mining, merging her company into KDYSFS and handing over the captive i'Falzin siblings, Nimzar and Eliza, along with all records of the planet Izin which the Empire will erase. After Sieara signs and departs, Scaven forces the siblings to choose which of them dies; Eliza volunteers herself, but Nimzar throws himself in front of the blaster and is killed. Scaven keeps the grieving Eliza as bait to lure her Jedi brother Ezkhil i'Falzin. Gavin Shai intervenes to take the girl somewhere safe, while a troubled Danik Kreldin warns Scaven not to underestimate the vengeful Jedi before leaving.

title: RPlog:Sean Dillon Returns to Etti IV
summary: Newly-promoted CSA Prex Simone Drake travels to the Capital City starport on Etti IV with her bodyguard Shikh and Espo escort to greet returning Executive Officer Sean Dillon, encountering the Imperial agent Alora Antieres who is still pressing for custody of Han Solo. At Piper's home, Dillon is briefed on how Chewbacca blasted through CSA fighters to kidnap Roark and on the deal struck with Grand Moff Caldny to hand Solo to the Empire. Dillon presses to lure Leia Organa Solo to Etti IV as leverage, but Piper firmly refuses to take part, and Dillon outwardly concedes; afterward Piper confides to Shikh her distrust of both Dillon and Alora.

title: RPlog:Second Battle of Mon Calamari, First Assault
summary: Former Imperial admiral Danik Kreldin leads a three-Star-Destroyer task force out of hyperspace at Mon Calamari to destroy the New Republic shipyards and the NRSD Crusader. Scimitar and TIE bombers make repeated runs on the shipyards while NR starfighter squadrons (Falcon, Ghost, Rogue) scramble to defend, and the capital ships trade fire. The Imperial fighter screen and bombers are gradually winnowed by skilled Republic pilots like Ikihsa Enb'Zik, Syyvos Rintari and Johanna's squad. Although the shipyards are damaged, they survive, and the Imperials ultimately fail to destroy the Crusader, forced toward withdrawal.

title: RPlog:Second Battle of Mon Calamari, Second Assault
summary: Danik Kreldin returns to Mon Calamari with a single Star Destroyer, the Conqueror, and escorts for a second assault after the repaired Crusader is back in service. Kreldin's plan is to isolate and destroy the NRSD Templar by pinning down the assault frigate Adamant and gunship Garm Bel Iblis with Moore's Fleet, while bombers under Lieutenant Biff pound the targets. The Adamant's crew fights to the last, keeping the Conqueror occupied, before its engines fail and it rams the Conqueror in a suicidal collision that severely damages the Star Destroyer. With the Conqueror crippled and the Crusader pressing the attack, Kreldin orders a withdrawal, vowing to next strike Corellia.

title: RPlog:Secret Service Supper
summary: During the ongoing Caspian Wargames, Sar Admiral Crestian Tarasar hosts an intimate dinner of glowblue noodles aboard his Interdictor flagship Leviathan for members of the Caspian Intelligence Service. Guests include Mandalorian agents Rawnie Qrowe and Nara Rhen, along with Zubindi Hakoon, Veruca Violet, and the young Atsvara. They discuss spycraft, the believability of Nara's deep-cover socialite persona, and Rawnie's covert technical intelligence-gathering on the new fighters. The evening culminates in Nara being promoted to Field Agent and given an advance of credits.

title: RPlog:Seductions
summary: At the Grand Palanhi Hotel, smuggler Paul Nighman is relaxing when Jessalyn Valios and the young Shenner arrive seeking to flee the planet and hide from the Black Dragon. The Dark Jedi Alora Antieres, accompanied by Crimthan and his Espos, corners Jessalyn and attempts to seduce her to the dark side, promising she could 'save' Luke Skywalker and bind him to her forever. Paul and Shenner physically drag the distraught Jessalyn out of the hotel and back to the Black Dragon, where Princess Leia talks her down from Alora's mental manipulation. Afterward Paul comforts Shenner and shares music with her, beginning to feel a renewed attraction.

title: RPlog:See Arissa Run
summary: Lured to Cloud City by a cryptic message promising information, the half-Echani privateer captain Arissa Kiiko walks into a trap set by Imperial Warlord Vassily Korolov, who is angry she accessed restricted Imperial files. The two engage in an extended Echani martial-arts duel with force pikes in the industrial hub, during which Korolov reveals his Sith abilities and toys with her, drawing blood.
